title: gilia aggregata subvar. arizonica
intro: Gilia aggregata subvar. arizonica, also known as Arizona gilia or globemallow, is a perennial plant that belongs to the figwort family and is native to the southwestern United States. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Arizona gilia include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and even support immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
